calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-23-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daryl Weinroth for (Executor) Britta Tomer.

Mr. Weinroth addressed the issue of additional compensation stating that they have been working on this estate for 9 years, that everything was handled quickly with the exception of a piece of real estate, that they tried to sell it for 9 years but were unsuccessful even with a drastically reduced price, that finally the executor agreed to purchase for $9,100.00, and that they have had to make numerous court appearances, deal with real estate agents, file status reports and accountings. Mr. Weinroth further stated that the extraordinary fees are justified by the amount of effort that has been put forth and that in regards to the travel time of 3.75 hours he will accept whatever the court feels is reasonable. The Court asked why a notice of proposed action was filed instead of a petition. Mr. Weinroth apologized for the incorrect procedure and stated that all parties received notice and that there were no objections. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Report And Petition For Continuance Of Administration
Off Calendar Nov-04-2013 Continued To Nov-03-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearance: Daryl Weinroth for Britta Tomer (Petitioner).

Mr. Weinroth reported on the problem with selling the lot in El Dorado County, that they have lower the price to $9,000.00 and still no offer. The Court suggested that giving it to charity. Mr. Weinroth stated that they will do everything they can to sell it and that he requested for continuance. The status of administration was continued to November 3, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status report. Mr. Weinroth to prepare an order for today's hearing.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Report And Petition For Continuance Of Administration
Off Calendar Oct-01-2013 Continued To Nov-04-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Report And Petition For Continuance Of Administration
Off Calendar Jan-22-2013 Continued To Oct-01-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Report And Petition For Continuance Of Administration
Off Calendar Jul-19-2011 Continued To Jan-22-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daryl Weinroth for and with Britta Tomer.

Mr. Weinroth requested that the Court put off the next status report until the El Dorado property sells stating that they lowered the price down to $14,750.00 and that it still won't sell. In addition, he stated that none of the Beneficiaries were interested in purchasing the property, that there was no need to abandon the property, that the taxes were only $300.00 per year, that there was no harm in keeping it and that it should sell within 18 months. The status report and petition for continuance of administration was continued to January 22, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Report And Petition For Continuance Of Administration
Off Calendar Jun-08-2011 Continued To Jul-19-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any party, counsel or interested person.

A Notice to Appear will be issued to the Executor and the Attorney to appear on July 19, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why a Status Report or a Petition for Continuance of Administration has not been filed. The matter was continued to July 19, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Bui.
